title: A novel UHPLC method for quantitative analysis of five anthraquinone derivatives in Asphodeline plant extracts
author: Lazarova, Irina
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description: A reverse-phase ultra-high-performance liquid chromatography (UHPLC) method was established in this study for the simultaneous determination of five anthraquinones in extracts of  Asphodeline  species. A gradient program was used for chromatographic separation of the components under investigation. The analytes were separated on a Waters Acquity BEH C18 column with a flow rate of 0.3 mL/min. The mobile phase, consisting of acetonitrile and water containing 0.1% (v/v) formic acid, was used to elute the components under study. The compounds were detected at 435 nm. The method was validated according to ICH guidelines with respect to specificity, precision, linearity, and robustness. The proposed method was found to be convenient, reliable, and accurate for the quantitative determination of the described anthraquinones in  Asphodeline  species.

The method was validated according to ICH guidelines with respect to specificity, precision, linearity, and robustness.
